curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 19 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry
Explain discovery of sub-atomic particles, atomic models, developments leading to Bohr's model
Explain genesis of periodic classification, modern periodic law, nomenclature for Z>100
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, Ionic/Electrovalent bond concepts
Analyze applications of thermodynamics, measurement of ∆U and ∆H
Analyze homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, ionic equilibrium in solution
Classify classical ideas vs. electron transfer concepts
Classify and analyze properties of Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes

topics: Properties, Measurement and Uncertainty in Measurement
Atomic Models and Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom

practicals.title: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork
Determination of melting point of an organic compound
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Determination of boiling point of an organic compound
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Crystallization of Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Determination of pH of solutions obtained from fruit juices, acids, bases, salts using pH paper or universal indicator
Comparing the pH of solutions of strong and weak acids of same concentration
Study the pH change in the titration of a strong base using a universal indicator
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions by changing concentration of either ion
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions by changing concentration of either ion
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of a given solution of Sodium hydroxide by titrating it against standard solution of Oxalic acid
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
Determination of strength of a given solution of hydrochloric acid by titrating it against standard Sodium Carbonate solution
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol (Lyophilic: starch/egg albumin/gum; Lyophobic: aluminum hydroxide/ferric hydroxide/arsenous sulphide)
Dialysis of sol-prepared in (a) above
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study of reaction rates: Reaction of Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide at room temperature using different concentration of Iodide ions
Study of reaction rates: Reaction between Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate using starch solution as indicator
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate
Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid (HCl) and strong base (NaOH)
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4)
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating it against a standard solution of Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate

Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-)
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, Cl-, CH3COO-)

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

practical_assessment.type: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Students (Classes XI & XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practicals): 10 Marks
notes: Student required to answer any 10 out of 15 questions.
Every question requires about two minutes to be answered.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any other investigatory project, which involves about 10 periods of work, can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
suggested_topics_class_xi: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
suggested_topics_class_xii: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

subtopics: Properties, Measurement and Uncertainty in Measurement
Atomic Models and Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom
key_concepts: Developments Leading to Quantum Mechanical Model
Quantum Mechanical Model of the Atom

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

practical_assessment.type: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Students (Classes XI & XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practicals): 10 Marks
notes: Student required to answer any 10 out of 15 questions.
Every question requires about two minutes to be answered.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any other investigatory project, which involves about 10 periods of work, can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
suggested_topics_class_xi: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
suggested_topics_class_xii: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, represent phenomena at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and apply knowledge to real-life situations.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to trace the development from sub-atomic particles discovery through Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model.
competencies: Explain discovery of sub-atomic particles, atomic models, developments leading to Bohr's model

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand periodic law, electronic configurations, block types, and trends in properties.
competencies: Explain genesis of periodic classification, modern periodic law, nomenclature for Z>100
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) and understand hybridization.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, Ionic/Electrovalent bond concepts

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes, calorimetry, spontaneity, Gibbs energy change, and equilibrium.
competencies: Analyze applications of thermodynamics, measurement of ∆U and ∆H

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand dynamic equilibrium, constants (K), reaction quotient (Q), Gibbs energy relationship, and factors affecting equilibria.
competencies: Analyze homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, ionic equilibrium in solution

competency_groups.title: Redox and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify oxidation/reduction in terms of electron transfer, assign oxidation numbers, and relate redox to electrode processes.
competencies: Classify classical ideas vs. electron transfer concepts

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds, classify them, apply IUPAC nomenclature, understand isomerism, reaction mechanisms, and purification techniques.
competencies: Apply structural representations, classification, nomenclature
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand properties of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, and assess carcinogenic/toxicity.
competencies: Classify and analyze properties of Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes

competency_groups.title: Solution Chemistry and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to express concentration, understand solubility, vapor pressure, ideal/non-ideal solutions, and determine molar mass via colligative properties.
competencies: Apply concepts of types of solutions, expression of concentration

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electrochemical cells, galvanic cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolytic cells, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion.
competencies: Analyze operation of Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze rate of reaction, factors influencing it, integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, and collision theory.
competencies: Apply concepts of rate determination and temperature effects

competency_groups.title: d-and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand position in periodic table, electronic configuration of d-block elements, general properties, important compounds, lanthanoids, actinoids.
competencies: Analyze general properties and applications of Transition Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds Theory
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply Werner's theory, understand nomenclature, isomerism, bonding (including metal carbonyls), and applications.
competencies: Apply definitions of terms pertaining to Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify, name, understand C-X bond nature, preparation methods (haloalkanes/haloarenes), physical properties, chemical reactions.
competencies: Analyze classification and nomenclature of halo compounds

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify compounds, apply nomenclature rules, understand structures of functional groups, and properties.
competencies: Differentiate between Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand nomenclature/structure of carbonyl/carboxyl groups, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions.
competencies: Analyze Preparation and Uses of Aldehydes/Ketones

competency_groups.title: Amines Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand structure, classification, nomenclature, preparation methods (including Diazonium salts), physical properties.
competencies: Analyze Chemical Reactions and Importance of Diazonium Salts

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify and understand the structure and function of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, hormones.
competencies: Identify components of Biomolecules

competency_groups.title: Problem Solving and Scientific Inquiry
competency_groups.description: Inculcate problem-solving skills, integrate life skills/values in context of chemistry, contribute to frontier research areas (climate change, environment), interface with other disciplines.
competencies: Apply acquired knowledge to solve problems in new situations

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

practicals.title: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork
Determination of melting point of an organic compound
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Determination of pH of solutions obtained from fruit juices, acids, bases, salts using pH paper or universal indicator
Comparing the pH of solutions of strong and weak acids of same concentration
Study the pH change in the titration of a strong base using a universal indicator
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions by changing concentration of either ion
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions by changing concentration of either ion
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of a given solution of Sodium hydroxide by titrating it against standard solution of Oxalic acid
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate
Determination of strength of a given solution of hydrochloric acid by titrating it against standard Sodium Carbonate solution
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol (Lyophilic: starch/egg albumin/gum; Lyophobic: aluminum hydroxide/ferric hydroxide/arsenous sulphide)
Dialysis of sol-prepared in (a) above
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study of reaction rates: Reaction of Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide at room temperature using different concentration of Iodide ions
Study of reaction rates: Reaction between Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate using starch solution as indicator
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate
Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid (HCl) and strong base (NaOH)
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4)
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating it against a standard solution of Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate

Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-)
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, Cl-, CH3COO-)
